mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative after FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which increases recession risk and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the return response is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in PAYEMS together with RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, which supports near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone in cross-validated hit rate, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in the top signal quartile.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO after a weak prior month signals industrial output stabilization and better supply-side momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after at least one prior monthly decline
failure_condition: Reject if rebounds in INDPRO are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect vanishes in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS tightens real financial conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS signals slowing employment growth and weaker aggregate income, which leads equity investors to mark down future earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for two consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than half of occurrences, or if the signal remains insignificant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both improve, residential activity and consumer spending jointly indicate stronger final demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to beat a single-proxy benchmark, or if top-quartile signals do not produce positive 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-driven disinflation phase, firm INDPRO accompanied by stable CPIAUCSL implies improving real output without margin pressure, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns do not concentrate in periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL, or if the interaction effect is unstable across rolling 3-year windows.
